text:
Market today: The mood is cautious with the Fed minutes showing a split on interest rates and some officials even pushing for a June hike. This is the real driver, as higher rates typically pressure growth-oriented tech and chip names, which heavily populate your book.

Your holdings: SOXL is a major concern, down over 31% as a leveraged ETF. SPAL, another leveraged ETF, is also deeply in the red at -26.28%. QQQI is flagged for a trim at -2.11% and needs attention near its stop. Remember, margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Keep a close eye on any further Fed comments regarding rate hikes. Also, monitor your leveraged ETFs for continued decay and volatility.

Bottom line: Prioritize active risk management on your leveraged and deeply negative positions to protect your margin.
plays: Your SOXL is down over 31% and is a leveraged ETF. Given the "SELL" status and margin trading, seriously consider cutting losses or significantly reducing this high-risk position.
With QQQI flagged for "TRIM" and nearing its stop at 52, be ready to reduce exposure if it hits that level.
Your new SPAL position is already down over 26% and is a leveraged ETF. Re-evaluate this high-risk holding, especially with margin amplifying losses.
